{"query":"SSPX six bishops excommunication 2026 Vatican schism decree official statement","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1957,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.americamagazine.org/vatican-dispatch/2026/07/02/vatican-excommunicates-sspx-bishops-priests-and-some-lay-faithful/","title":"Vatican declares excommunicated SSPX bishops, warns priests and lay faithful of schism - America Magazine","content":"The Vatican, as widely expected, issued on July 2 an unequivocal decree declaring that six bishops of the Priestly Fraternity of Saint Pius X involved in the episcopal ordination ceremony in Switzerland yesterday are in schism and automatically excommunicated.
The decree concludes by issuing a stern warning to the some 600,000 members of the SSPX, saying: “The clerics and the lay faithful are warned not to adhere to the schism of the Priestly Fraternity of St."},{"url":"https://www.catholicworldreport.com/2026/07/02/vatican-confirms-excommunication-of-sspx-bishops-declares-schism/","title":"Vatican confirms excommunication of SSPX bishops, declares schism – Catholic World Report","content":"Pius X (SSPX) consecrated four bishops without the permission of Pope Leo XIV, the Vatican issued a decree declaring the excommunication of all bishops involved in the ceremony and stating that the group is in schism.
The Vatican Secretary of State, Cardinal Pietro Parolin, later called the SSPX’s act “schismatic”.
No, the DDF just now excommunicated them and declared schism.
Vatican I (1868-1870) was interrupted in 1870 by the Italian Army overtaking of the Papal states.
In the early 1960’s Saint Padre Pio warned that Satan had entered the bosom of the church."},{"url":"https://dianemontagna.substack.com/p/holy-see-declares-sspx-bishops-excommunicated","title":"Holy See Declares SSPX Bishops Excommunicated After July 1 Consecrations","content":"VATICAN CITY, 2 July 2026 — The Holy See today issued a decree declaring the excommunication of the bishops involved in the episcopal consecrations carried out by the Priestly Fraternity of Saint Pius X (SSPX) on 1 July.
From the Palace of the Dicastery, 2 July 2026"},{"url":"https://cruxnow.com/vatican/2026/07/sspx-declared-to-be-in-schism-clerics-excommunicated","title":"Crux","content":"The declaration came from the Dicastery for the Doctrine of the Faith, just one day after the group illicitly made four men bishops without papal approval and in defiance of repeated warnings from senior Vatican officials and even the pope himself.
The DDF decree declares that the bishops involved in the illicit consecrations are excommunicated, while an attached explanatory note states that clerics and faithful who “formally adhere” to the SSPX schism have likewise incurred excommunication.
The highly technical language of the Thursday decree from DDF essentially means the ordinations performed Tuesday at the SSPX seminary facility in Écône, Switzerland, were by law (if not by their very nature) schismatic – a formal and deliberate injury to the unity of the Church."},{"url":"https://aleteia.org/2026/07/02/rome-confirms-excommunication-of-6-sspx-bishops/","title":"Open menu","content":"Rome confirms excommunication of 6 SSPX bishops
The decree then formally declares: “Bishop Alfonso de Galarreta, Pascal Schreiber, Michael Goldade, Michel Poinsinet de Sivry and Marc Hanappier have incurred ipso facto the excommunication latae sententiae reserved to the Apostolic See.” As for Bishop Bernard Fellay — excommunicated once before in 1988 alongside three other bishops consecrated by Archbishop Marcel Lefebvre, an excommunication lifted by Benedict XVI in 2009 — the decree states that having “participated directly in the liturgical celebration as co-consecrator, thereby publicly adhering to the schismatic act, he has incurred the excommunication latae sententiae provided for by canon 1364 §1 of the CIC 2021.”
Those who attend SSPX Masses without consciously rejecting papal authority are in a different canonical situation."}]}

The decree warned clergy and laypeople that they would also incur the","score":0.8446273,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.ncronline.org/vatican-declares-sspx-bishops-priests-schismatic-says-lay-faithful-risk-excommunication","title":"Vatican declares SSPX bishops, priests schismatic, says lay faithful risk excommunication | National Catholic Reporter","content":"Excommunicated Bishop Alfonso de Galarreta elevates the host during an illicit consecration Mass in Écône, Switzerland, celebrated by the Society of St. Pius X on July 1, 2026. As hundreds of lace-laden priests processed into an ornate Mass July 2 on a hillside in the Swiss Alps, the bishops leading the line, their clergy and the 6,000 lay faithful bowed in prayer around them were declared members of a schismatic group no longer in communion with the Catholic Church. Less than 24 hours after the ultraconservative Society of St. Pius X created four new bishops in open defiance of Pope Leo XIV, the Vatican declared that those bishops, the men who consecrated them and the priests and lay faithful who adhered to the schism, had been formally excommunicated from the church.","score":0.7969415,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.61,"request_id":"c644e125-8e90-404b-896d-56c266211479"}
